text:
Modern public policymaking increasingly relies on data-driven insights derived from massive datasets generated by digital services, mobile applications, and social media. While machine learning (ML) and artificial intelligence (AI) offer unprecedented opportunities to automate evidence-based decision-making, their integration into the public sector faces substantial technical, legal, and ethical hurdles. Crucially, AI-driven policies must ensure transparency and explainability to gain public trust and comply with emerging regulatory frameworks like the European Union’s AI Act.

This paper addresses these challenges by proposing a comprehensive technological and operational framework for trustworthy AI in policymaking. Our contributions include: (i) a reference architecture tailored for AI-based policy development; (ii) a cloud-based Virtual Policy Management Environment (VPME) designed for the seamless implementation of data-driven policies; (iii) an innovative ML framework that prioritizes the creation of interpretable models through rule-based explanations; and (iv) practical guidelines for aligning AI deployments with legal compliance and risk management standards. We demonstrate the practical utility of these solutions through real-world validation cases involving local governments, spanning domains such as smart parking, infrastructure maintenance, and water management. The results indicate that explainable AI can achieve high performance while providing the transparency necessary for accountable governance.
